school.name: The Schoolhouse
school.description: Each teacher at the schoolhouse leads a different class in a different room. Work out who teaches what, and where.
clues: 83% goes with Ellis if and only if either Period 1 goes with Annex or Period 4 goes with Nash.
Bungalow goes with at most one of Ellis, 79%, and Period 4.
Either Carver goes with Bungalow or 83% goes with Period 2.
Either 77% goes with Period 5 or 79% goes with Library.
Between Library and Workshop, one goes with Ames and the other with Frost.
Ellis, Frost, and Period 1 go with Bungalow, Library, and 83%, in some order.
Period 4 goes with at least two of Ames, Studio, and 81%.
81% goes with exactly two of Carver, Bungalow, and Period 4.
Exactly one of these is true: Nash goes with Period 2; Library goes with 85%; Studio goes with Period 3.

hints.text: Suppose Ellis went with Library — following the clues from there hits a contradiction, so it doesn't.
chain: Start by assuming Ellis goes with Library.
Library goes with Ellis, so Ames can't go with Library.
Library goes with Ellis, so Frost can't go with Library.
…but now the clue “Between Library and Workshop, one goes with Ames and the other with Frost” can no longer hold — a contradiction.
So Ellis does not go with Library.

chess.name: The Chess Club
chess.description: Regulars at the chess club each swear by a different opening, a favourite tactic, and a prized set. Work out who plays what.
clues: 1808 goes with Magnus if and only if either 5th goes with Benoni or 2nd goes with Vera.
Caro-Kann goes with at most one of Magnus, 1708, and 2nd.
Either Garry goes with Caro-Kann or 1808 goes with 4th.
Either 1658 goes with 1st or 1708 goes with French.
Between French and Pirc, one goes with Anatoly and the other with Mikhail.
Magnus, Mikhail, and 5th go with Caro-Kann, French, and 1808, in some order.
2nd goes with at least two of Anatoly, Italian, and 1758.
1758 goes with exactly two of Garry, Caro-Kann, and 2nd.
Exactly one of these is true: Vera goes with 4th; French goes with 1858; Italian goes with 3rd.

hints.text: Suppose Magnus went with French — following the clues from there hits a contradiction, so it doesn't.
chain: Start by assuming Magnus goes with French.
French goes with Magnus, so Anatoly can't go with French.
French goes with Magnus, so Mikhail can't go with French.
…but now the clue “Between French and Pirc, one goes with Anatoly and the other with Mikhail” can no longer hold — a contradiction.
So Magnus does not go with French.
